Operation 'recordConsent' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is a mere repetition of the summary with no actionable details. Added clarity on what inputs are expected (ConsentRecord schema), what is returned (created record with ID), and side effects (audit trail creation). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: Record user consent preferences and choices
data.description_suggestion: Records a user's consent preferences for specified purposes, categories, or vendors. Accepts a ConsentRecord object containing the subject identifier, consent choices, timestamp, and applicable purposes. Creates a new consent record in the system and returns the created record with a unique consentId. This operation establishes an audit trail for compliance purposes.
data.reasoning: Original description is a mere repetition of the summary with no actionable details. Added clarity on what inputs are expected (ConsentRecord schema), what is returned (created record with ID), and side effects (audit trail creation). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'getConsent' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description lacks specificity about inputs (consentId parameter) and outputs (ConsentRecord structure). Enhanced to clarify the retrieval mechanism, return value structure, and use cases for AI agent decision-making.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve consent record by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a specific consent record by its unique consentId. Returns the complete ConsentRecord object including the subject identifier, consent choices, timestamp, and applicable purposes. Use this operation to audit or verify a user's consent status for a particular consent event.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs (consentId parameter) and outputs (ConsentRecord structure). Enhanced to clarify the retrieval mechanism, return value structure, and use cases for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'getConsentBySubject' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary and description are clear and complete. They explain the input parameter (subjectId), output (collection of ConsentRecord objects), and use cases. AI agents can easily determine when to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Retrieve consent records for a subject
data.current_description: Retrieves all consent records associated with a specific data subject. Accepts a subjectId parameter and returns a collection of ConsentRecord objects representing the subject's complete consent history. Useful for subject access requests or consent verification workflows.
data.reasoning: Summary and description are clear and complete. They explain the input parameter (subjectId), output (collection of ConsentRecord objects), and use cases. AI agents can easily determine when to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Submit a data subject access request
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Data Subject Request (DSR) for privacy rights such as access, deletion, or portability. Accepts a DSRRequest object containing the subject identifier, request type, and supporting details. Returns the created DSRRequest with a unique dsrId and initial status. Initiates the DSR workflow and triggers compliance processing according to applicable regulations.
data.reasoning: Original description is a bare repetition with no operational context. Added clarity on DSR types (access, deletion, portability), input schema details, return value with ID, and side effects (workflow initiation). data.summary_suggestion: Update data subject request status or details
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing data subject request (DSR) identified by dsrId. Allows modification of DSR status, priority, or other request details. Requires authentication via API key. Returns the updated DSR object with all current fields. Side effects include updating the DSR's modification timestamp and potentially triggering status-dependent workflows.
